title: Understanding Average Acceleration
text: To find the average acceleration, you need to know the change in velocity and the time over which this change occurs. The formula for average acceleration is: \( a $$=$$ \frac{\Delta v}{\Delta t} \).

title: Convert Units
text: The speed is given in $$\frac{km}{s}$$, but we need it in $$\frac{m}{s}$$ for the calculation. Remember that $$1$$ km $$=$$ $$1000$$ $$m$$.

title: Calculate Change in Velocity
text: Convert the final speed from $$\frac{km}{s}$$ to $$\frac{m}{s}$$. What is the final speed in $$\frac{m}{s}$$?

title: Calculate Average Acceleration
text: Use the formula \( a $$=$$ \frac{\Delta v}{\Delta t} \) with \( \Delta v $$=$$ $$6500$$ \) $$\frac{m}{s}$$ and \( \Delta $$t$$ $$=$$ $$60$$ \) s. What is the average acceleration?

title: Express in Multiples of g
text: To express the acceleration in terms of \( g \), divide the average acceleration by \( $$9.80$$ \) m/s².

title: Calculate Multiples of g
text: Divide the average acceleration by \( $$9.80$$ \) m/s². What is the acceleration in terms of \( g \)?
